name: vflags literal column length exceeds remaining buffer
description: Byte 0x36: structured mode (bit 7=0), short(len=5) consumes 5 bits (x=1, n(3)=5), then vflags mode=10 (literal bit-stream, mode=2) takes 2 bits, exhausting the byte. The decoder calculates maxBits = 1×8 − 8 = 0 remaining bits, but needs len=5 flag bits. Since 5 > 0, it MUST refuse with a column-overflow error. Bit layout: struct(0) | short_x=01 | short_val=101 | vflags_mode=10.

name: string length field exceeds buffer bounds
description: Derived from enc({x:'hello'})=0ad4c58a6c2f4b2d00 by setting bits 1–0 of byte 4 to 11 (0x6c→0xec). Those two bits lie in a string-length sub-field; the corrupted value claims a string length larger than the remaining buffer, which MUST be refused.

name: key-length field exceeds buffer bounds
description: Derived from enc({a:1,b:2})=12d28b144d372042 by setting bit 3 of byte 0 to 1 (0x12→0x1a). Bit 3 of byte 0 falls within the short() field that encodes the structured-mode entry count; setting it inflates the declared count beyond the true number of encoded entries. When the decoder reaches the key-data section for the phantom trailing entry, the key length it reads from the buffer exceeds the remaining payload, which MUST be refused.
